rule.desc: Always use type hints for function parameters, return values, and class attributes.
rule.rationale: Type hints improve code clarity, enable static analysis, catch bugs early, and serve as documentation.

example.code: # Good def parse_transaction(line: str, date_format: str = "%Y-%m-%d") -> Transaction: """Parse a transaction line into a Transaction object.""" ...
# Bad def parse_transaction(line, date_format="%Y-%m-%d"): """Parse a transaction line into a Transaction object.""" ...

rule.tool_support: mypy strict mode requires type hints. ruff rule ANN enforces annotations.
